slides.title: Places, People, and Plans
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 9: Places, People, and Plans. Welcome to Unit 9 — the final unit of Year 4! This unit is all about places, people, and plans. You will learn the words we use to talk about travelling: the airport, the station, the museum, the capital city, and much more. You will meet the people we find in busy places, like the customer at a shop and the workers in an office. Most of all, you will learn how to make plans — how to invite a friend, how to say what you are going to do, and how to give clear directions. By the end, you will be able to plan a whole trip in English, just like Selma and Isaac do in this unit's story when they explore the city of Mombasa. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name many kinds of places — the airport, station, museum, mall, and more
Talk about the people and movement in busy places, using words like customer and arrive
Invite someone politely using would like to
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name many kinds of places — the airport, station, museum, mall, and more. you will also talk about the people and movement in busy places, using words like customer and arrive. you will also invite someone politely using would like to. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: tourism, airport, station, railroad, capital, nation. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes A Trip to the Capital and Living Near the Equator. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Would like to
bullets: “Would like to” is a polite way to say what you want, or to invite someone to do something. It is a kinder, softer way of saying “I want to”. We use it a...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Would like to. “Would like to” is a polite way to say what you want, or to invite someone to do something. It is a kinder, softer way of saying “I want to”. We use it a lot when we are being friendly and respectful. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1: A Tour Plan for Your Capital City
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Invite a Friend. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
